title: abronia umbellata subsp. insularis
intro: Abronia umbellata subsp. insularis, also known as the island desert marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Nyctaginaceae family. It is native to the Channel Islands of California and is prized for its bright yellow and orange flowers, which bloom from March to July. The island desert marigold has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been found to have potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
